response.justification: The candidate brings genuine geospatial domain knowledge through founding GISCollective and building GIS applications, plus real systems-level experience writing low-latency DLang services handling millions of requests per second on Linux. However, the role's primary requirement is modern C++ (or Rust), and his professional C++ experience is a single seven-month stint ending in 2015; his recent work is predominantly TypeScript/Node.js full-stack development. He has no routing, tiling, or navigation guidance experience specifically, so while the domain adjacency and systems background make him worth a conversation, he is not a strong direct fit.
strengths: Founded and maintains GISCollective, an open-source geo-data platform, demonstrating hands-on geospatial data processing experience
Low-latency, high-throughput systems work at Dunnhumby/Sociomantic using async I/O, fibers, and caching on Linux
Strong engineering craftsmanship: TDD, CI/CD, testing tooling authorship, and comfort learning across the stack
concerns: Professional C++ experience is brief (~7 months) and dated (2015), with no Rust to compensate for a C++-primary role
No experience with routing engines, map tiling pipelines, or navigation guidance systems
Recent roles focus on web/full-stack TypeScript product work rather than large-scale data pipeline or distributed systems engineering

key_factors.factor: Stale/limited modern C++ and no Rust
